stops.title: Upton Mansion
stops.description: Historic mansion and cultural landmark in Upton, useful as a route opener for neighborhood memory and institution-building | Day: Day 1 | Time: Late Morning | Location: 811 W Lanvale St, Baltimore, MD 21217
stops.assetNeeded: neighborhood intro card; corridor orientation overlay

stops.title: Pennsylvania Avenue Main Street Gateway
stops.description: Gateway block for the Black Arts & Entertainment District and a strong orientation stop for the avenue's creative corridor | Day: Day 1 | Time: Noon | Location: 1700 Pennsylvania Ave, Baltimore, MD 21217
stops.assetNeeded: district overview card; route timeline labels

stops.title: Billie Holiday Statue
stops.description: Public memorial honoring Billie Holiday and the avenue's deep musical legacy | Day: Day 1 | Time: Early Afternoon | Location: 1400 Pennsylvania Ave, Baltimore, MD 21217
stops.assetNeeded: performance-era photo card; jazz lineage annotations

stops.title: Royal Theatre Marker
stops.description: Marker for the Royal Theatre, a defining venue in Baltimore's Black performance history | Day: Day 1 | Time: Early Afternoon | Location: 1329 Pennsylvania Ave, Baltimore, MD 21217

stops.title: The Avenue Bakery
stops.description: Contemporary corridor anchor that ties the avenue's history to present-day neighborhood gathering and entrepreneurship | Day: Day 1 | Time: Mid Afternoon | Location: 2229 Pennsylvania Ave, Baltimore, MD 21217
stops.assetNeeded: corridor continuity card; neighborhood voice quotes

stops.title: Arch Social Club
stops.description: Historic Pennsylvania Avenue club tied to Black arts, performance, and civic gathering in Baltimore | Day: Day 1 | Time: Late Afternoon | Location: 2426 Pennsylvania Ave, Baltimore, MD 21217
stops.assetNeeded: street-sign card; corridor overlay; oral history pull quotes

cardMedia.alt: Baltimore Tours draft route badge for harbor labor and freedom stories.

stops.title: President Street Station
stops.description: Rail station and Civil War-era landmark that helps connect Baltimore's freedom stories to movement, escape routes, and political tension | Day: Day 1 | Time: Mid Afternoon | Location: 601 S President St, Baltimore, MD 21202
stops.assetNeeded: rail corridor overlay; freedom route annotations

stops.title: Douglass Place
stops.description: Rowhouses associated with Frederick Douglass in Fell's Point, tying built environment to labor, freedom, and Black urban life on the waterfront | Day: Day 1 | Time: Mid Afternoon | Location: 520 S Dallas St, Baltimore, MD 21231

stops.title: Ship Caulkers' Houses
stops.description: Historic houses at 612-614 South Wolfe Street that interpret Baltimore's Black ship caulkers and working-class maritime life in Fell's Point | Day: Day 1 | Time: Mid Afternoon | Location: 612-614 S Wolfe St, Baltimore, MD 21231
stops.assetNeeded: house overlay; caulker trade card; waterfront labor timeline

stops.title: Frederick Douglass-Isaac Myers Maritime Park
stops.description: Waterfront site connecting Black maritime labor, entrepreneurship, and freedom stories in Fell's Point | Day: Day 1 | Time: Late Afternoon | Location: 1417 Thames St, Baltimore, MD 21231
stops.assetNeeded: waterfront overlay; dock labor scene card; shipyard annotations
